Title: Glutathione contributes to resistance responses to TMV through a differential modulation of salicylic acid and reactive oxygen species

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced in inoculated leaves at 12 and 24 h after TMV-GFP infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced in inoculated leaves at 12 and 24 hpi after TMV-GFP infection; elevated in OE-10 and OE-18 transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TMV-GFP infection; VIGS silencing; transgenic overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core two-step glutathione biosynthetic pathway, first step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent ligation of L-glutamate and L-cysteine to form γ-glutamylcysteine, the first intermediate in glutathione biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor pool for the target product glutathione
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported; BSO specifically inhibits ECS and NbECS manipulation strongly alters GSH level and virus resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Redox buffering, ROS detoxification/homeostasis, activation of SA-mediated defense, enhancement of local and systemic resistance to TMV
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inoculated leaves (12 hpi) and systemic upper leaves (5 dpi)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GSH accumulation, reduces ROS and oxidative damage, enhances SA signaling, and increases resistance to TMV; silencing causes the opposite phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; TRV-VIGS silencing; constitutive overexpression in transgenic lines; GSH quantification; TMV-GFP fluorescence imaging; RT-qPCR; western blotting; ROS and MDA/electrolyte leakage assays; SA measurement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious visible phenotype reported in silenced plants or chemical-treated plants under the tested conditions; knockout inferred likely lethal from prior literature, not this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NbECS sequence showed similarity to tomato SlGSH1 template used for cloning; overexpression alone was sufficient to increase endogenous GSH and antiviral resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to enhance antiviral resistance by elevating GSH biosynthesis; may be stacked with SA-pathway defense traits while monitoring redox balance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TMV infection induced NbECS expression and increased GSH content. 2. VIGS silencing of NbECS lowered GSH, increased TMV accumulation, ROS, MDA, and electrolyte leakage, and reduced SA, NbICS1, NbNPR1, NbPR1, NbPR2, and NbPR5 expression. 3. Constitutive overexpression of NbECS increased GSH, reduced oxidative damage and ROS, enhanced SA pathway and ROS-scavenging gene expression, and increased resistance to TMV.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced in inoculated leaves at 12 and 24 h after TMV-GFP infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced in inoculated leaves at 12 and 24 hpi after TMV-GFP infection
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core two-step glutathione biosynthetic pathway, second step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent addition of glycine to γ-glutamylcysteine to produce glutathione
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key for end-product formation; strong control inferred from silencing phenotype, but rate-limiting not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Redox buffering, ROS detoxification/homeostasis, activation of SA-mediated defense, enhancement of local and systemic resistance to TMV
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inoculated leaves (12 hpi) and systemic upper leaves (5 dpi)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GSH biosynthesis and resistance to TMV; silencing lowers GSH, elevates ROS and oxidative damage, suppresses SA signaling, and increases TMV accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; TRV-VIGS silencing; GSH quantification; TMV-GFP fluorescence imaging; RT-qPCR; western blotting; ROS and MDA/electrolyte leakage assays; SA measurement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious visible developmental phenotype reported after silencing under the tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NbGS was cloned using tomato SlGSH2 as template reference; only loss-of-function-type evidence by VIGS was presented in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to boost glutathione-dependent antiviral defense, though this paper provides only silencing evidence and no transgenic overexpression test
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TMV infection induced NbGS expression and coincided with increased GSH accumulation. 2. VIGS silencing of NbGS reduced GSH content and compromised both local and systemic resistance to TMV. 3. NbGS silencing increased ROS and oxidative damage and reduced SA accumulation and expression of NbICS1, NbNPR1, NbPR1, NbPR2, and NbPR5.
